016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.rest.client.assertion;
018
019import java.io.*;
020
021import org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.*;
022import org.apache.juneau.rest.client.*;
023
024/**
025 * A fluent assertion class for HTTP response bodies.
026 *
027 * <p>
028 * Obtain instances via {@link ResponseAssertion#body()}.
029 *
030 * <p>
031 * <b>Beta — API subject to change:</b> This type is part of the next-generation REST client and HTTP stack
032 * ({@code org.apache.juneau.marshall.ng.*}).
033 * It is not API-frozen: binary- and source-incompatible changes may appear in the <b>next major</b> Juneau release
034 * (and possibly earlier).
035 *
036 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
037 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/NextGenRestClient">juneau-ng REST client</a>
038 * </ul>
039 *
040 * @since 9.2.1
041 */
042@SuppressWarnings({
043   "resource" // Eclipse resource analysis: response is borrowed; caller closes it
044})
045public final class ResponseBodyAssertion {
046
047   private final RestResponse response;
048   private final ResponseAssertion parent;
049
050   ResponseBodyAssertion(RestResponse response, ResponseAssertion parent) {
051      this.response = response;
052      this.parent = parent;
053   }
054
055   /**
056    * Asserts that the response body (as a UTF-8 string) equals the expected value.
057    *
058    * @param expected The expected body string. May be <jk>null</jk> to assert an empty/absent body.
059    * @return This object (for chaining).
060    * @throws AssertionError If the body does not match.
061    * @throws UncheckedIOException If reading the body throws {@link IOException}.
062    */
063   @SuppressWarnings({
064      "java:S1201" // 'equals(String)' is an intentional fluent-assertion DSL method (asserts body equality), not an override of Object.equals(Object); renaming would break this public (beta) API.
065   })
066   public ResponseBodyAssertion equals(String expected) {
067      var actual = readBodyAsString();
068      if (expected == null ? actual != null : !expected.equals(actual))
069         throw new AssertionError("Expected body '" + expected + "' but got '" + actual + "'");
070      return this;
071   }
072
073   /**
074    * Asserts that the response body (as a UTF-8 string) contains the given substring.
075    *
076    * @param substring The substring to look for. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
077    * @return This object (for chaining).
078    * @throws AssertionError If the body is absent or does not contain the substring.
079    * @throws UncheckedIOException If reading the body throws {@link IOException}.
080    */
081   public ResponseBodyAssertion contains(String substring) {
082      var actual = readBodyAsString();
083      if (actual == null || !actual.contains(substring))
084         throw new AssertionError("Body '" + actual + "' does not contain '" + substring + "'");
085      return this;
086   }
087
088   /**
089    * Asserts that the response body is empty (null or zero-length).
090    *
091    * @return This object (for chaining).
092    * @throws AssertionError If the body is non-empty.
093    * @throws UncheckedIOException If reading the body throws {@link IOException}.
094    */
095   public ResponseBodyAssertion isEmpty() {
096      var actual = readBodyAsString();
097      if (StringUtils.isNotEmpty(actual))
098         throw new AssertionError("Expected empty body but got '" + actual + "'");
099      return this;
100   }
101
102   /**
103    * Asserts that the response body is present and non-empty.
104    *
105    * @return This object (for chaining).
106    * @throws AssertionError If the body is null or empty.
107    * @throws UncheckedIOException If reading the body throws {@link IOException}.
108    */
109   public ResponseBodyAssertion isNotEmpty() {
110      var actual = readBodyAsString();
111      if (StringUtils.isEmpty(actual))
112         throw new AssertionError("Expected non-empty body but body was absent or empty");
113      return this;
114   }
115
116   /**
117    * Asserts that the response body starts with the given prefix.
118    *
119    * @param prefix The expected prefix. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
120    * @return This object (for chaining).
121    * @throws AssertionError If the body is absent or does not start with the prefix.
122    * @throws UncheckedIOException If reading the body throws {@link IOException}.
123    */
124   public ResponseBodyAssertion startsWith(String prefix) {
125      var actual = readBodyAsString();
126      if (actual == null || !actual.startsWith(prefix))
127         throw new AssertionError("Body '" + actual + "' does not start with '" + prefix + "'");
128      return this;
129   }
130
131   /**
132    * Returns the parent {@link ResponseAssertion} to continue assertion chaining.
133    *
134    * @return The parent assertion. Never <jk>null</jk>.
135    */
136   public ResponseAssertion and() {
137      return parent;
138   }
139
140   private String readBodyAsString() {
141      try {
142         return response.getBodyAsString();
143      } catch (IOException e) {
144         throw new UncheckedIOException(e);
145      }
146   }
147}
